creator: bdrewery@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 2013-12-02 15:43, John Baldwin wrote:
> On Wednesday, November 27, 2013 11:55:28 am Bryan Drewery wrote:
>> Below are failed ports. They look unrelated.
>> 
>> http://package21.nyi.freebsd.org/bulk/9mfc-default-pr_184173/2013-11-24_15h50m53s/logs/errors/xqilla-2.3.0_1,1.log
>> http://package21.nyi.freebsd.org/bulk/9mfc-default-pr_184173/2013-11-24_15h50m53s/logs/errors/sfslite-shdbg-1.2.7_1.log
>> http://package21.nyi.freebsd.org/bulk/9mfc-default-pr_184173/2013-11-24_15h50m53s/logs/errors/sfslite-shared-1.2.7_1.log
>> http://package21.nyi.freebsd.org/bulk/9mfc-default-pr_184173/2013-11-24_15h50m53s/logs/errors/sfslite-1.2.7_1.log
>> http://package21.nyi.freebsd.org/bulk/9mfc-default-pr_184173/2013-11-24_15h50m53s/logs/errors/isic-0.07_2.log
> 
> Hmm, to be clear, you think these failures are due to other changes 
> since your
> last 9.x run and it is ok for me to proceed with my patch?

I did 2 exp-runs. One with your patch and the other without. Usually I 
do it from the same base system but on this I accidentally ran one on a 
newer stable/9 than the other. So it's possible none of these are 
related. I leave it to you to analyze to determine that and commit when 
you feel it is safe.

-- 
Regards,
Bryan Drewery
